nodes.label: AI as automation for routine tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI as unhelpful or laborious for non-routine tasks
nodes.grounding_span: AI is very useful for automating routine and repeat tasks

nodes.label: AI as drafting assistant for emails
nodes.label_negative: AI as producing low-quality drafts requiring heavy editing
nodes.grounding_span: I will enter relevant details into say ChatGPT and ask for a draft before editing it

nodes.label: AI as augmentation tool for unfamiliar domains
nodes.label_negative: AI as unnecessary for familiar tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I will enter more detail and this often happens in areas where I am less experienced

nodes.label: AI as time-saving thinking starter
nodes.label_negative: AI as time-wasting due to instruction overhead
nodes.grounding_span: I often just want a basic draft so as to move away from a blank page

nodes.label: AI reliability and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: the AI isnt capable enough for this, nor is it accurate enough

nodes.label: AI as tool for lower-skilled or non-native speakers
nodes.label_negative: AI as inferior to own writing for skilled users
nodes.grounding_span: for people with lower ability that the AI may well have surpassed their own writing skills

nodes.label: AI search and procurement capability
nodes.label_negative: AI search lacking depth and judgment
nodes.grounding_span: I have tried in vain to try and make AI models produce very good search results in specific procurement scenarios

nodes.label: AI as narrow task performer vs. holistic understanding
nodes.label_negative: AI as capable of overarching goals and granular steps
nodes.grounding_span: AI is very good at very narrow tasks but struggles with the overall picture

nodes.label: Personalized AI instruction frameworks
nodes.grounding_span: it almost needs a training module so that individual's can consistently instruct AI using a similar set of instructions

nodes.label: Productivity and time efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: it has greatly reduced the time spent in undertaking accounting and bookkeeping functions

nodes.label: Quality and accuracy of work output
nodes.grounding_span: the copy is of fairly low quality compared to my own standards

nodes.label: Skill maintenance and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: If you did not know these skills where AI looks certain to take over, it would make using AI and instructing AI much more difficult

nodes.label: Personalized and adaptive tool use
nodes.grounding_span: it needs to be an organic learning curve from individuals that can be retained and learnt from

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for routine automation
nodes.grounding_span: I find this an excellent use of AI

nodes.label: Frustration with AI search limitations
nodes.grounding_span: I find this frustrating because I can see how it could be improved but the models just dont want to play ball

nodes.label: Cautious reliance on AI for drafting
nodes.grounding_span: I do edit a lot - however, when I draft an email and come back and edit my own work, it will usually involve re-writing 30% or so

nodes.label: Optimism about AI's potential with personalized training
nodes.grounding_span: I think there is a big opportunity to make AI work in your favour very well

nodes.label: Task-dependent delegation — adjusts AI use based on task familiarity and complexity
nodes.grounding_span: This is dependent on the type of email

nodes.label: Comparative evaluation — judges AI output against own standards and others' abilities
nodes.grounding_span: the models produce results that are better than they can create themselves
